name: Mocha Macadamia Decadence
recipeIngredient: Bittersweet or semisweet chocolate, chopped (3 ounces)
Granulated instant espresso or coffee (2 teaspoons)
Bittersweet or semisweet chocolate, finely chopped (4 ounces)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Butter an 8-inch round cake pan with 2-inch sides and line the bottom of the pan with parchment.
2. Stir chopped chocolates in a double boiler over simmering water until melted and smooth. Cool slightly.
3. Process macadamia nuts and flour in a food processor until the nuts are finely ground. Transfer this mixture to a small bowl; do not clean the food processor.
4. Add butter and sugar to the food processor and blend using on/off turns until the mixture is smooth.
5. Add eggs, cognac or brandy, instant espresso (or coffee), and vanilla extract and process until smooth.
6. Add melted chocolate and process until just combined.
7. Add nut mixture and process using on/off turns until the mixture is just combined. Transfer batter to the prepared pan and smooth the top with a spatula.
8. Bake until a toothpick inserted halfway between the edge and center of the cake comes out clean, about 40 minutes. Cool the cake in the pan on a rack for 30 minutes.
9. Invert the cake onto a rack; remove the parchment and cool completely.
10. For the glaze, bring cream to a boil in a heavy small saucepan. Remove from heat and add chocolate and stir until melted and smooth. Stir in corn syrup. Let the glaze stand until cool enough to spread, about 20 minutes.
11. Transfer the cake to a rack. Spread the glaze over the top and sides of the cake. Refrigerate until set. Transfer the cake to a platter and let it stand at room temperature for 1 hour before serving.
